summary: nolimit-x: obfuscated mass-spam / SMS-bombing / mailbox-scraping toolkit
details: nolimit-x ships its entire codebase (46 files) as obfuscator.io-packed JavaScript hidden in a dotfile directory `.ad/`, with `package.json` `main` set to `.ad/x0.js` and keywords including `red-team`, `email`, `sender`, `smtp`. The CLI implements mass email sending, SMS bombing via SMTP-to-MMS carrier gateways (Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile, Cricket), OAuth device-code flows that scrape Microsoft Graph contacts/inbox/sent and Google contacts from authorized victim mailboxes, and on-the-fly DKIM keypair generation (`crypto.generateKeyPair('rsa')`). Every byte of the helper modules (`sms-providers.js`, `phone-validator.js`, `smtp-health-cache.js`, `web-command.js`, `x0.js`-`xz.js`) is wrapped in a 968-entry rotated `_0x` string array with an RC4+base64 decoder and a self-defending wrapper that throws on regex tampering — packing intended to defeat review, not to protect IP. The package is purpose-built abuse tooling against third parties (mailbox owners, mobile-carrier SMS gateways, recipients of forged mail) rather than a library. There is no `npm install` lifecycle harm and no auto-execution on `require` (the CLI exits without a subcommand), but distribution of an obfuscated abuse toolkit through the npm registry is itself an ecosystem-integrity concern and any developer or build system that pulls this dependency inherits a hidden, deliberately unreviewable codebase that performs unauthorized access to third-party mail/SMS infrastructure when invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
